content:
Let \(p=23\).  Note that \(\gcd(a,391)\) can be \(1,17\) or \(23\), so the largest possible value of \(\gcd(a,391)\) is \(23\), and the smallest possible \(\gcd(a,b)\) is \(1\).  Thus the inequality

   lcm(a,b) > lcm(a,391)
⇔ b⋅gcd(a,391) / gcd(a,b) > 391

is most easily met when gcd(a,391)=23 and gcd(a,b)=1, giving the condition

   b⋅23 > 391   ⇔   b > 391/23 = 17.

Hence the smallest integer \(b\) that works is \(b=18\).  For instance, taking \(a=23\) (so gcd(23,18)=1 and gcd(23,391)=23) gives

   lcm(23,18)=23·18=414
   lcm(23,391)=391,

and indeed \(414>391\).  Therefore the minimum \(b\) is

\boxed{18}.

message.content:
Let \(p=23\).  Note that \(\gcd(a,391)\) can be \(1,17\) or \(23\), so the largest possible value of \(\gcd(a,391)\) is \(23\), and the smallest possible \(\gcd(a,b)\) is \(1\).  Thus the inequality

   lcm(a,b) > lcm(a,391)
⇔ b⋅gcd(a,391) / gcd(a,b) > 391

is most easily met when gcd(a,391)=23 and gcd(a,b)=1, giving the condition

   b⋅23 > 391   ⇔   b > 391/23 = 17.

Hence the smallest integer \(b\) that works is \(b=18\).  For instance, taking \(a=23\) (so gcd(23,18)=1 and gcd(23,391)=23) gives

   lcm(23,18)=23·18=414
   lcm(23,391)=391,

and indeed \(414>391\).  Therefore the minimum \(b\) is

\boxed{18}.
